children.heading: .—Advisory and Assistance Services Defined
children.label: a .—Advisory and Assistance Services Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “advisory and assistance services” has the meaning given such term in .section 1105(g) of title 31

Subject to the requirements of this section, , and other applicable law, the head of an agency may enter into a task order contract (as defined in ) for procurement of advisory and assistance services.section 3406 of this titlesection 3401 of this title
The head of an agency may enter into a task order contract for procurement of advisory and assistance services only under the authority of this section.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Contract Period
children.label: c .—Limitation on Contract Period
children.text: The period of a task order contract entered into under this section, including all periods of extensions of the contract under options, modifications, or otherwise, may not exceed five years unless a longer period is specifically authorized in a law that is applicable to such contract.

children.heading: .—Content of Notice
children.label: d .—Content of Notice
children.text: The notice required by and section 8(e) of the Small Business Act () shall reasonably and fairly describe the general scope, magnitude, and duration of the proposed task order contract in a manner that would reasonably enable a potential offeror to decide whether to request the solicitation and consider submitting an offer.section 1708 of title 4115 U.S.C. 637(e)

children.heading: Required Content of Solicitation and Contract.—
children.label: e Required Content of Solicitation and Contract.—

children.heading: .—Solicitation
children.label: 1 .—Solicitation
children.text: The solicitation for the proposed task order contract shall include the information (regarding services) described in .section 3403(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Contract
children.label: 2 .—Contract
children.text: A task order contract entered into under this section shall contain the same information that is required by paragraph (1) to be included in the solicitation of offers for that contract.

children.heading: .—Authority to make multiple awards
children.label: 1 .—Authority to make multiple awards
children.text: The head of an agency may, on the basis of one solicitation, award separate task order contracts under this section for the same or similar services to two or more sources if the solicitation states that the head of the agency has the option to do so.

children.label: A
children.text: provide for a multiple award authorized under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: include a statement that the head of the agency may also elect to award only one task order contract if the head of the agency determines in writing that only one of the offerers is capable of providing the services required at the level of quality required.

children.heading: .—Nonapplication
children.label: 3 .—Nonapplication
children.text: Paragraph (2) does not apply in the case of a solicitation for which the head of the agency concerned determines in writing that, because the services required under the task order contract are unique or highly specialized, it is not practicable to award more than one contract.

children.heading: .—Increase in scope, period, or maximum value of contract only by modification of contract
children.label: 1 .—Increase in scope, period, or maximum value of contract only by modification of contract
children.text: A task order may not increase the scope, period, or maximum value of the task order contract under which the order is issued. The scope, period, or maximum value of the contract may be increased only by modification of the contract.

children.heading: .—Use of competitive procedures
children.label: 2 .—Use of competitive procedures
children.text: Unless use of procedures other than competitive procedures is authorized by an exception in subsection (a) of and approved in accordance with subsection (e) of such section, competitive procedures shall be used for making such a modification.section 3204 of this title

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: 3 .—Notice
children.text: Notice regarding the modification shall be provided in accordance with and section 8(e) of the Small Business Act ().section 1708 of title 4115 U.S.C. 637(e)

children.label: A
children.text: the award of a follow-on contract has been delayed by circumstances that were not reasonably foreseeable at the time the initial contract was entered into; and

children.label: B
children.text: the extension is necessary in order to ensure continuity of the receipt of services pending the award of, and commencement of performance under, the follow-on contract.

children.heading: .—Limit of one extension
children.label: 2 .—Limit of one extension
children.text: A task order contract may be extended under the authority of paragraph (1) only once and only in accordance with the limitations and requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: .—Inapplicability to Certain Contracts
children.label: i .—Inapplicability to Certain Contracts
children.text: This section does not apply to a contract for the acquisition of property or services that includes acquisition of advisory and assistance services if the head of an agency entering into such contract determines that, under the contract, advisory and assistance services are necessarily incident to, and not a significant component of, the contract.
